title: Three sentences
instruction: Write three things before you sleep. What drained you. What gave you something. What your body was doing that your words were not. No analysis. Just what is true.
note: If you miss a night, do not catch up. Pick up the next evening.

title: Energy log
instruction: Each day this week, note one thing that cost you more than expected and one that gave you more than expected. At the end of the week look at both lists. What pattern do you see?
note: One week only. Then you have your map.

title: Instinct record
instruction: Once a day, note a moment where you had a read on something before you had a reason for it. What was the instinct. What did you do with it. Were you right.
note: The point is not to act on every instinct. The point is to start noticing them.

title: Name it precisely
instruction: Once a day, stop and find the exact word for what you are feeling. Not fine. Not stressed. The exact word. If you cannot find it, write what it is closest to.
note: Precision here is the skill. Vague words keep feelings vague.

title: One real thing
instruction: Say one honest thing to one of the two people at work you already identified as safe. Not small talk — something real. About their work, about the day, about something you noticed. Once.
note: You do not explain the gap. You just end it.

title: Fairness inventory
instruction: Write down three situations from the last few years where you were treated unfairly. What happened. What you concluded about yourself as a result. Which of those conclusions are you still carrying.
note: This is not about grievance. It is about seeing clearly what got installed.

title: Map the real structure
instruction: This week, watch rather than engage. Who do people actually go to when something needs to happen — not who they should go to. Where does information move and where does it stop. Write what you see.

title: One sentence
instruction: Before your next meeting, decide what you want to say. One sentence. Prepare it in advance. Say it. Then stop — do not explain it or immediately follow it up.
note: The stopping is the harder part.

title: Values from evidence
instruction: Write your actual values — not aspirational ones. What have you chosen when it cost you something. That list is your anchor. Keep it somewhere you can return to when something pulls you off centre.
note: If you cannot evidence it, it is not on the list yet.

title: Stillness practice
instruction: Once a day in a moment of pressure — feet flat, hands still, hold eye contact gently. Not performance. Just choosing not to move. Notice what happens.
note: This is physical and it is trainable. It gets easier.

title: One small bet
instruction: Once a week, act on an instinct in a low-stakes situation before you talk yourself out of it. Small. Recoverable if wrong. The point is to prove to yourself that you can move.
note: Low stakes only. You are building evidence, not taking risks.

title: Presence walk
instruction: Choose a room this week you would normally enter quietly. Decide where you are going to sit before you walk in. Walk to it. Sit down. Take up your actual space.
note: Where you sit. How you move. This is physical and trainable.

title: Rule audit
instruction: Pick five rules in your workplace — written or unwritten. For each: is this load-bearing, meaning real consequences if broken, or discretionary, meaning selectively enforced. Decide your approach to each.
note: Understanding a system is not becoming it.

title: The non-reaction
instruction: This week, when something provokes you — pause one second longer than feels natural. Respond to what was actually said, not the tone it was said in. Afterwards write what happened.
note: One second longer. That is the whole practice.

title: No-fear audit
instruction: Write three things you are currently avoiding. For each: what do you think will happen if you stop. What is the realistic probability. Could you survive it if it did happen.
note: Most of what we avoid costs more in the avoidance than it would in the doing.
